Container.Name: pia-qbittorrent
Container.Overview:
![Version](https://img.shields.io/github/v/tag/GeorgeAL78/pia-qbittorrent-docker?label=version&color=blue)
qBittorrent combined with Private Internet Access (PIA) VPN, built on Alpine Linux for a minimal footprint.
Features: • WireGuard and OpenVPN • Kill switch (IPv4 + IPv6) and DNS leak protection • PIA port forwarding for better seeding • Correct UID/GID file ownership for Unraid • Configurable UMASK • Multi-arch (amd64 + arm64)
Fork of j4ym0/pia-qbittorrent with bug fixes and extra features. Leave the Network Type on Bridge (the Unraid default) — the VPN tunnel runs inside the container.
Pair with the Windows 11 desktop app: https://github.com/GeorgeAL78/qbittorrent-desktop

Config.Name: Port Forwarding
Config.Description: Enable PIA port forwarding for better seeding. Falls back gracefully if your region does not support it (e.g. US)

Config.Name: UID
Config.Description: User ID for qBittorrent process. Use 99 for Unraid nobody

Config.Name: GID
Config.Description: Group ID for qBittorrent process. Use 100 for Unraid users

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: Umask for downloads. 000=fully open (recommended for Unraid), 002=group-writable, 022=owner only

Config.Name: Web UI Port Variable
Config.Description: Must match the port mapping above

Config.Name: Legacy IPTables
Config.Description: Use legacy iptables instead of nftables

Config.Name: Extra Subnets
Config.Description: Comma-separated extra subnets allowed through the kill switch. Use this if your *arr apps or reverse proxy are on a different Docker network and cannot reach qBittorrent e.g. 172.18.0.0/16

Config.Name: Allow Local Subnet Traffic
Config.Description: Set to true to allow LAN devices to connect directly to the container
